summary: Lock progress bar and navigation buttons to neon-green color, regardless of page accent color

currentImplementation.progressBar: Styled in resources/css/screen/swiper.css line 25-29. Uses `linear-gradient(90deg, var(--gradient-left) 0%, var(--gradient-right) 100%)` via `.swiper-pagination-progressbar .swiper-pagination-progressbar-fill`. The --gradient-left defaults to --accent-color and --gradient-right defaults to --color-blue in :root, but both change per accent color class (.blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, .green) defined in colors.css lines 209-237.
currentImplementation.navigation: Styled in resources/css/screen/swiper.css lines 32-94. Uses `linear-gradient(90deg, var(--gradient-left) 0%, var(--gradient-right) 100%)` on `.carousel-nav-btn::before` pseudo-element for hover fill. Same --gradient-left/--gradient-right tokens that vary by accent color.
currentImplementation.sharedStyles: CRITICAL: The styles in swiper.css are shared by CardsCarousel, DynamicContentCarousel, TestimonialsCarousel, and GalleryCarousel. Changes must be scoped to .dynamic-content-carousel only.

neonGreenValues.notes: gradientLeft = --color-neon-green = rgba(212,255,69,1). gradientRight = --color-blue = rgba(71,203,242,1). These are the default neon-green gradient pair from :root in colors.css. We can also use the Tailwind theme tokens --color-neon-green and --color-blue to stay DRY.

fileChanges.description: Add scoped CSS overrides to lock gradient and accent colors to neon-green values

designDecisions: Use CSS custom property overrides scoped to .dynamic-content-carousel rather than hard-coded hex values, so if the neon-green palette is ever updated in colors.css, this block automatically picks up the change.

Do NOT modify resources/css/screen/swiper.css because it is shared by 4+ carousel blocks (CardsCarousel, GalleryCarousel, TestimonialsCarousel, DynamicContentCarousel). Modifying it would break color theming for the other carousels.
The override only needs --gradient-left and --gradient-right because both the progress bar fill and the nav button hover ::before background use the same gradient custom properties.
No PHP changes needed -- the .dynamic-content-carousel class is already applied to the section wrapper in both DynamicContentCarousel.php (line 94) and DynamicContentCarousel.tsx (line 364).
No JS changes needed -- the color is purely CSS-driven.

qaDetails.testOn: A page using a non-neon-green accent color (e.g., blue, purple, orange) that contains a DynamicContentCarousel block
verify: Progress bar gradient shows neon-green-to-blue (not the page accent color)
Navigation button hover fill shows neon-green-to-blue gradient (not the page accent color)
Other carousel blocks on the same page (if any) still respect the page accent color
Autoplay toggle button hover also shows neon-green gradient (uses same .carousel-nav-btn class)
